summary:
**Claim**: A p-value less than 0.10 indicates that the observed effect is unlikely to occur by chance under the null hypothesis.  
**Evidence**: - The p-value quantifies the probability of obtaining results at least as extreme as those observed if the null hypothesis (no effect) were true. - A p-value < 0.10 implies such outcomes would happen in fewer than 10% of repeated experiments under no real effect.  
**Answer**: C

(487 tokens)

All critical nuances preserved without redundancy or oversimplification.
